Today the neighbourhood is popular with families, students, artists and well-known personalities of the German cultural scene. Within 10 min walking you are also in the trendy “Mitte“ area, where you will find beautiful cafes, restaurants, bars and art galleries. The closest subway is U Senefelderplatz. Please ask for our list of locally curated recommendations around the neighbourhood.

neighborhood_overview:
You are in a quiet location with easy access to the city center. The German-Russian Museum and Tierpark Berlin are in the immediate vicinity.

The city can be reached quickly by subway.
In addition to the good connection, the Rheinsteinpark is practically opposite and therefore also a very nice playground. The park is a well-kept and beautiful green area.
host_about:
Hi ich bin der Christian, aktuell 34 Jahre alt und wohne in Berlin, wenn wir nicht gerade mit unserem Camper durch die Gegend flitzen oder auf Reisen sind.

Da ich selbst gerne mit meiner Frau reise und wir viel unterwegs sind, finden wir es super spannend solche tollen Erlebnisse für andere bieten zu können. Wir sind vielleicht noch an vielen Dingen am Lernen und es mag nicht immer perfekt sein, aber für Feedback und Vorschläge sind wir immer zu haben und teilen diese auf unseren Trips auch immer gerne :)

Meine Frau kommt ursprünglich aus Lettland und ich aus dem beschaulichen Brandenburg, leben wir jetzt gemeinsam in Berlin und finden die Orte um Berlin in Brandenburg super.

Da wir viel auch in der ganzen welt unterwegs sind, kann es natürlich auch mal sein dass ich nicht immer sofort dran bin wenn es doch mal etwas zum Austauschen gibt. Genau deswegen habe ich zum Glück ja alles super für dich/euch automatisiert, sodass du dich mega wohlfühlen kannst und eine tolle Zeit hast.

Ansonsten sind wir gerne mit dem Rad unterwegs oder einfach schön entspannt zu Fuß Wandern in der Natur. 

Für jegliche Anregungen und Feedback sind wir sehr offen und würden uns freuen mit euch in Kontakt zu treten, wenn wir helfen können. Oft vermutlich aus der Ferne, da wir ja selbst viel unterwegs sind.

neighborhood_overview: Zehlendorf, located in the southwest of Berlin, offers a serene escape from the city's bustling center. Known for its leafy streets, elegant villas, and proximity to the Grunewald Forest, Zehlendorf provides a tranquil living environment. The district boasts a mix of historical charm and modern amenities, with local shops, cafes, and restaurants catering to a diverse community. Zehlendorf is renowned for its excellent schools and cultural attractions, including the AlliiertenMuseum, showcasing Berlin's post-WWII history. The nearby Wannsee lake offers recreational activities, making Zehlendorf an ideal choice for those seeking a balance between urban conveniences and natural beauty. Public transportation, including S-Bahn and U-Bahn connections, ensures easy access to the heart of Berlin.
host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

There is a car free 15 km path at the water front starting in front of the house, going over a bridge, through a park and into a forest.

With some distance, there are young people in the area, clubs are in the area.

There are building sites around. Sometime you will hear machine sound. Over the holiday time it will be still, though.

description:
We are offering a spacious room in our unique and homely Neukölln apartment.

The apartment is located in a vibrant and creative neighborhood, with countless restaurants, cafes, bars, galleries, and parks; all walking distance away.

We are also just 5 minutes away from the U-Bahn station Rathaus Neukölln, with easy transport links to the new BER Airport.

neighborhood_overview:
Within walking distance from the apartment:
Five minutes to Weserstraße, well known for its bars and cafes.
Five minutes to Rixdorf, a small village in the heart of Neuköln.
Twenty minutes along the canal to the famous Maybachufer Market.
Twenty minutes to Tempelhof, the abandoned airport.

Bikes are also very easy to rent in Berlin. There are several bike rental apps available, offering flexibility, as you can rent/return the bikes almost anywhere in the city.

A short train ride away:
Alexanderplatz 20 minutes
East Side Gallery 30 minutes
Brandenburger Tor 30 minutes

There are several supermarkets nearby, the closest is on the opposite side of the road. There is also a great Späti (famous Berliner off-licence) a 3 minute walk away, with a great selection of beers.

neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Prenzlauer Berg, a centrally located and family friendly neighborhood in northeast Berlin. There are cafes and restaurants throughout the area, including notable international options like Wen Cheng Handpulled Noodles. Anyone who likes to cook will be pleased by the selection of local supermarkets and Prenzlauer Berg hosts a popular Sunday farmer's market at Mauerpark. The MACHmit! Museum for Children and the Zeiss Planetarium are both located in the neighborhood. Prenzlauer Berg is served by several U-Bahn and bus lines.
host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

name: Central, cosy and elegant flat
description: This flat in Prenzlauer Berg, completed in 2021, is ideally located between Volkspark Friedrichshain and Velodrom, 30 min. from the airport and 10 min. from the centre. neighborhood_overview:
Due to the location in a side street, all main sources of traffic noise are at least 200 m away and it is very quiet, especially at night.
In the daytime on weekdays, schoolchildren play on the other side of the street during breaks, but this is only perceptible when the windows are open.
The large and beautiful Volkspark Friedrichshain can be reached on foot in 5 minutes, the park at the Velodrom even faster, another park and quiet areas for walking as well as supermarkets (LIDL, REWE, DM) and pharmacies in 10-15 minutes.

name: Lekkerurlaub Kreuzberg - Family Room
description:
best location
gorgeous mattresses
wonderful guests
neighborhood_overview:
Our hostel is located in downtown Berlin, within the S-Bahn ring and yet far away from the well-trodden paths of the tourists. The central location between the trendy clubs of Friedrichshain-Kreuzberg and the former Tempelhofer Feld airport - by the way one of the largest inner city open spaces in the world - is the perfect starting point for discovery tours to all the well known sights of the city. The subway station Schönleinstraße is only a 5 minutes walk away, the ride with underground line 8 to Alexanderplatz takes less than 10 minutes.

The Graefekiez, the area around Graefestraße at Kreuzberg, borders at the hip district of Neukölln. The neighbourhood is characterised by beautiful 19th century houses, a rather mixed population, nice cafés and bars, many small new shops, interesting galleries and restaurants. The Landwehr Canal with its banks Maybach and Paul Lincke Ufer is only a stone's throw away. Here you can sit relaxed on the shore.

Or how about a steam
host_about:
Die Räume einer ehemaligen Erste- Hilfe- Stelle in Kreuzberg wurden zu einem Gästehaus ausgebaut, der alte Charme blieb erhalten. Eingerichtet mit Objekten, die im Berliner Straßenleben zurückgelassen wurden, wie alte Hölzer, Türen und Fenster, sowie Flohmarktfundstücken.
Das Hostel "Lekkerurlaub" mit 8 Zimmern für maximal 20 Gäste ist inhabergeführt.
Ebenerdig gelegen, betrittst du unseren Aufenthaltsraum, daran schließen sich die Zimmer, Duschen und WCs an.

Berlinbücher, Reiseführer und Stadtpläne stehen zur Verfügung.
W-Lan ist frei.
Da wir keine 24 Stunden Rezeption haben, bitten wir darum, die Check- in Zeiten zu respektieren.

Solltest du dich verspäten, gib uns umgehend per Mail oder Telefon Bescheid.
Auf der Straße sind öffentliche, kostenlose Parkplätze verfügbar.
Bei schönem Wetter kannst du deinen Kaffee auf typische Berliner Art auf dem Trottoir vor der Tür genießen.

neighborhood_overview:
Prenzlauer Berg is no doubt one of the most mythical districts of the city.
It embodies Berlin’s cultural heritage from diverse historical periods, proletarian and middle-class styles of the fin-de-siècle, just as the imprint of socialism.
What is more, there has always been a tradition of resistance and opposition giving birth to the spirit of creativity that incessantly surrounds you here.
The architecture should hold attention because in spite of the galloping renovation since the fall of the Wall, it still retains some of scars from the Second World War.

host_about:
Das W22 Hotel am Kurfürstendamm empfängt Sie mit seinem besonderen Charme.
 

Entdecken Sie ein schillerndes Ambiente zwischen Kitsch und Gemütlichkeit in einem gediegenen Berliner Altbau. Der ideale sehr zentrale Ausgangspunkt, um die turbulente Hauptstadt Berlin zu erkunden. Ein Ruhepol für gestresste, geschäftige Reisende, Startpunkt für Flaneure und Stadtentdecker, Basis für Kultur-, Kunst- und Modeinteressierte, Spielraum für Mütter, Väter und Kinder.
 
Die geräumigen Zimmer auf drei Etagen überraschen mit einer phantasievollen Gestaltung. In der 1. Etage warten bspw. eine Burg, eine Ranch, ein Spielzimmer und Schneewittchen auf die kleinen Gäste. Ein Aufzug aus den 20iger Jahren führt vom Frühstücksraum in die Suite für Romantiker.
 

Helle, ruhige Zimmer: alle mit TV und Bad, einige mit Badewanne, einige mit Balkon, aber alle mit Minibar, Telefon, kostenlosem W-LAN.
 

Frühstücksbuffet und Kinderbetreuung zusätzlich buchbar.


Sehenswürdigkeiten in der Nähe: Schloss Charlottenburg, Deutsche Oper, Schaubühne, Theater am Kurfürstendamm, Zoo Berlin, das KaDeWe und viele Galerien. Fußläufig sind der bekannte 'Thaipark', verschiedene Cafés, Restaurants, Boutiquen und andere Einkaufsmöglichkeiten.
 

Das Hotel verfügt über exzellente Verkehrsanbindungen zur Messe Berlin, zum Flughafen, allen Fernbahnhöfen und zum ZOB. In der Nähe sind: U-Bahn, S-Bahn, Bus- und Taxi-Haltestand. 


Wir wünschen Ihnen einen schönen Aufenthalt und senden liebe Grüße!
 
Ihr W22 Hotel Team

name: Apartment: Berlin für Insider, Downtown am See
description: Welcome to Berlin-Tegel, the unknown jewel of the capital! Only 6 bus stops and 18 minutes by subway from the vibrant Friedrichstraße, a modern apartment with bathroom, fully equipped kitchen and terrace awaits you here! Ideal for business travelers, it offers a quiet oasis in the middle of the city with a swimming lake 300m away as well as shopping facilities, cafés and restaurants. Experience the best of both worlds: urban living in a green setting.
neighborhood_overview: Berlin-Tegel is part of the "city center" of Berlin with the subway connection (U6), because further north, still within the former city borders ("Mauer"), there are other districts (e.g. Hermsdorf and Frohnau) that can only be reached by bus or S-Bahn. The apartment is located in Tegel-Süd, a quiet residential area that is ideal for walks and relaxation. Berliners who prefer the quiet and green side of Berlin live here. At the end of the street (300m) is a forest with a bathing lake (formerly called Flughafensee). Only 1 km away is the Tegeler See, from here you can take a steamboat trip through the whole city and move through the city on the waterways of Berlin.

name: Friedrichshain - Charming and full of light
description:
This charming apartment is in a historical building with high ceilings very close to Boxhagener Platz and Simon-Dach-Strasse. Lots of restaurants in walking distance and you can get everywhere in Berlin easily by public transport (10 minutes walk to S-Bahn, 5 minutes to U-Bahn).
Ideal for couples, single travelers, business travelers and families with children older than 13.
neighborhood_overview:
As anyone from Friedrichshain will tell you, this district is not just the coolest in Berlin, but the hippest location in the entire universe. It has long been famed for its diverse cultural life, its experimental alternative lifestyles and the powerful spell it exercises on young people from across Germany. When it comes to club culture, Friedrichshain is now out in front – with southern Friedrichshain particularly ranked as home to the highest density of clubs in the city.
Along the Spree’s northern and southern banks, locals and visitors applaud the street musicians on the Oberbaumbrücke bridge ready to rock the world’s concert venues tomorrow. The borough is a stage and home for creativity – and not only when it comes to music. Here, artists create free spaces for their work – on the streets, in backyard flats and ateliers.

You will also encounter a variety of nice bars just around the corner. My tip for you: have a beer at „Mauerwerk“ in Zwinglistraße.

If you don’t want to walk you can also take a boat ride and discover Berlin from the water. Closest stop is Hansabrücke (12min by foot).

description:
Our Einfach Minilofts are 23sqm studio apartments ideal for up to 2 people.

neighborhood_overview: Located on the southern end of Friedrichstraße, we are just 3 minutes walk from Checkpoint Charlie, but in quiet surroundings in front of Besselpark. The main points of interest in Berlin like the Brandenburg Gate, Reichstag and Kanzleramt, are just a few train stops away, and the vibrant districts of Kreuzberg and Bergmannkiez are right at your doorstep. Looking over the city from inside Miniloft Kreuzberg, guests have an overview of some architectural history of 20th-century Berlin: The apartment blocks from the 60s and 70s and the post-modern buildings of the Berlin IBA in 1987 including the standout residential tower by John Hejduk, the Rocket-Tower by Sauerbruch/Hutton with the "pillbox" on the east side of the building, the apartment blocks on Leipziger Strasse and the sparkling Axel Springer skyscraper. You can even see the iconic tv tower of Alexanderplatz. The location of Miniloft Kreuzberg is a bermuda triangle for architect buffs!

host_about:
Hola, Salut, Hello! 
Some years ago I started to try nocarz as a guest, now hosting as well our apartment whenever we are abroad.  I love to travel, meet and greet new people from all over the world.  We are open minded, love arts, nature and international experiences.  The sound of music in the streets  in any city, village or beach promenade  makes us spontaneously dance and sing sometimes.  We love to live on the sunny side. ...
We, thats my eight year old daughter and and my little baby son and me.  We come from Germany, Berlin and speak German, English, Spanish and a little bit of French.  I am a Highschool teacher of Fine Arts  and English Language.  I do translations and  counseling as a second trait.  
We are looking forward to meet you, either as a guest or as a host
 : ) :):) 
Saludos, salut, regards, viele Grüsse, Tatjana with Josef and Rosaria


Hallo, ihr Lieben, 
da bin ich wieder. Vor Jahren habe ich schon ein paar mal nocarz gemacht, gemietet, vermietet, besucht, und gereist .... Die meisten Erfahrungen mit nocarz waren wirklich toll! Jetzt bin ich wieder da, als Gastgeberin und wir reisen derzeit weiter ... Wir - das sind meine siebenjährige Tochter Rosaria und mein 14 Monate alter Sohn Josef. Wir lieben es, neue Menschen, fremde Kulturen und andere Sprachen kennen zu lernen. Wir sprechen Englisch und Spanisch. Ein ganz kleines bisschen Französisch. Wir kommen aus Berlin, lieben unsere Stadt, Multi-kulti, Kunst und Kultur,
 aber wir mögen auch ruhige Orte, Natur, Sonne und Strand, Wüste, Regenwald, Berge, Vogelgezwitscher und Regenwürmer.
Unsere eigene Wohnung zu Hause in Berlin haben wir mit viel Sorgfalt und Liebe hergerichtet, kindgerecht, familien und -besucherfreundlich. Wir gehen als Gäste so mit unserer Unterkunft um, wie wir es uns auch von unseren eigenen Gästen wünschen: Hinterlassen es genauso sauber und unversehrt, wie wir sie vorgefunden haben - und wenn wir eine Spur hinterlassen, dann einen lieben Zettel an den Gastgeber mit unserem Namen, unsere Adresse und der Einladung, uns gerne auch einmal besuchen zu kommen. 
Wir freuen uns,  Euch kennen zu lernen!
Saludos, 
Tatjana mit Rosaria und Jose(URL HIDDEN)

description:
Hello,

You can/you can use our approx. 55 sqm apartment with balcony if you are looking for a central overnight stay with direct connection in Berlin (tram & subway about 200 m away, 2 stops to Alexanderplatz/3 to Gesundbrunnen). Mauerpark as well as the Mauersegler-Flohmarkt are about a 5-minute walk.
A pull-out sofa bed (suitable for 2 people) is available next to the bedroom. For everything else, just get in touch/get in touch :)
host_about:
.. Hallo, 

die Wohnung zeichnet sich vor allem durch ihre Lage aus. 
Ihr schafft es von der Wohnungstür innerhalb von 20 Minuten zum Hauptbahnhof, ins Regierungsviertel, zum Alexanderplatz....

Ich wohne hier mit meiner Freundin, weshalb auch ein Doppelbett zur Verfügung steht. Wenn man nicht in einer Pärchenkonstellation zu zweit unterwegs ist, kann auch die Couch ausgezogen und als Schlafplatz genutzt werden.  

Die Schönhauser Allee, die Max-Schmeling-Halle, der Rosenthaler Platz (eine U-Bahn Haltestelle), die Kulturbrauerei (z.B. mit dem DDR Alltagsmuseum) oder der Arconaplatz bieten viele Möglichkeiten der Freizeitgestaltung. 

Wenn ihr Fragen habt und/oder Ideen bzw. Anregungen braucht, dann meldet euch einfach bei uns. 
Anreisezeiten mit Schlüsselübergabe können wir individuell absprechen.. Unter Umständen können wir euch auch vom Alexanderplatz/Hauptbahnhof/Gesundbrunnen abholen und in die Wohnung begleiten. 
Grundsätzlich ist die Wohnung eine Nicht-Raucher-Wohnung - auf dem Balkon kann darf aber geraucht werden. 

Viele Grüße :)

Suite.030 hat sich auf die Vermietung von möblierten High-Class Apartments in Berlin spezialisiert, für kurz und mittelfristige Aufenthalte. Jedes Apartment ist handverlesen, erfüllt die höchsten Ansprüche und offiziellen Sterne-Kritieren und wird von uns rundum betreut.

name: Großzügiges Dachstudio in traumhafter Lage
description:
Fantastic attic studio in a quiet green location on the Wannsee is waiting for a guest or two!
After extensive sightseeing in the Berlin city, you can relax in the natural surroundings. In contrast to the bustling city center, Berlin-Kladow offers a haven of peace on the banks of the Havel, which widens to the Wannsee. You can walk endlessly on the waterfront. A special attraction is the BVG ferry via the Wannsee.
neighborhood_overview:
Kladow is a wonderful place to relax and unwind. Many people come from far away to enjoy this Berlin idyll - you can live in the middle of it. Whether by bike or on foot, the landscape of the Havel offers pure nature.
Starry skies, nighttime silence, and a breath of fresh air make for the special feel-good effect. In summer there are lakes for swimming very close to the accommodation. Nearby Sakrower Lake is one of the cleanest lakes in all of Brandenburg.
In the Kladower bike shop you can rent bikes and e-bikes.
The village structure has been preserved here. In Kladow there are many restaurants , cafes, banks and shopping, and yet life here is a quieter walk than in the hectic, crowded city.
Potsdam is close by and can be reached from Kladow by bus, by car or by bicycle.

name: Miniloft Kreuzberg Apartment Hotel: Vielfach
description:
Our exquisite Vielfach Minilofts have many highlights: a woodstove, two balconies, panorama windows with views of Berlin‘s skyline at 180 degrees.
Ideal for up to 4 people, their 53 sqm feature a large bedroom with a king sized bed (180 x 200 cm) and an east facing balcony, a living room with two sofas that convert into single beds, and an open kitchen with a large table for working or dining. The ensuite bathroom with shower has two doors, connecting to the living room and bedroom.
neighborhood_overview: Located on the southern end of Friedrichstraße, we are just 3 minutes walk from Checkpoint Charlie, but in quiet surroundings in front of Besselpark. The main points of interest in Berlin like the Brandenburg Gate, Reichstag and Kanzleramt, are just a few train stops away, and the vibrant districts of Kreuzberg and Bergmannkiez are right at your doorstep. Looking over the city from inside Miniloft Kreuzberg, guests have an overview of some architectural history of 20th-century Berlin: The apartment blocks from the 60s and 70s and the post-modern buildings of the Berlin IBA in 1987 including the standout residential tower by John Hejduk, the Rocket-Tower by Sauerbruch/Hutton with the "pillbox" on the east side of the building, the apartment blocks on Leipziger Strasse and the sparkling Axel Springer skyscraper. You can even see the iconic tv tower of Alexanderplatz. The location of Miniloft Kreuzberg is a bermuda triangle for architect buffs!

A lovely neighborhood to explore with colorful facades and 19th-century townhouses.

The loft is super centrally located on the border of the center of Mitte and Prenzlauer Berg. It is situated next to Kastanienallee, 10 minutes walk to the bustling Rosenthaler Platz, Weinberg Park and Arkonaplatz (the flea market on Sundays is not to be missed! It’s the arty fleamarket of Berlin. It is small but boasts all manner of retro furniture, clothing, records and books. A nice place to relax on a weekend afternoon), 15 minutes to shopping in Hackescher Markt and only 5 minutes to Mauerpark (where every Sunday is a popular meeting place for artists and a great hipster flea market) and Prater (berlin's first beer garden). The Museum island is only a few minutes walk away.
The area has numerous great restaurants, cafés, bars and shops

name: Mit Stil in Berlin
description: This particular home has its own style.
neighborhood_overview: Pleasantly elegant, but not exclusive area, full of bars and restaurants with outdoor tables, gardens and trees. Friedbergstrasse is one of the few streets that still retain the Berlin structure and atmosphere of the early 1900s.
host_about:
Ich komme aus Rom und lebe mit meiner Partnerin in dieser schönen Wohnung im Herzen von Berlin! Wir leben sehr gern hier! Die Mischung aus einem lebendigen Kiez und Ruhe, alles fußläufig, mit dem Rad oder den öffentlichen Verkehrsmitteln erreichbar, Kino, Kultur, Theater in der Nähe und in 10 Minuten im Grünen!
Das ist für uns Lebensqualität!
Was erwartet dich?
Die Friedbergstraße befindet sich im Herzen von Charlottenburg im "Stuttgarter Platz Kiez"! Sehr zentral, lebendig und dennoch sehr ruhig kannst du hier alle Vorzüge der Großstadt genießen. In der Nähe befinden sich viele Restaurants, Cafés und Läden.
Da du alle wichtigen Sehenswürdigkeiten von Charlottenburg aus in kurzer Zeit mit den öffentlichen Verkehrsmittels erreichen kannst, würde wir dir dies auch empfehlen. Somit umgehts du die Parkplatzsuche in der Innenstadt und siehst ganz entspannt viel mehr von Berlin!
Wir werden dich persönlich begrüßen und können dir nach deiner Ankunft gern einige Tipps geben.
Beste Grüße
Paolo Maria und Petra

neighborhood_overview: Novela is located in the leafy corner of Charlottenburg Wilmersdorf. It’s mellow and relaxed, but anything but secluded—you’ve got the park at Oliviaerplatz right across the street, and the bustling shopping boulevard of Ku’Damm just around the corner. Whether you fancy staying local and visiting bars, restaurants, or taking a stroll in the park, Wilmersdorf has it all. Adenauerplatz subway station is a 10-minute walk and Olivaer Platz/ Xantener Strasse bus stop is a 3-minute walk away! The area’s filled with small grocers, trendy cafes and good eating, so best to arrive with an empty stomach. And if you’re smelling fresh bread in the morning, it’s not your imagination—Novela is above one of Berlin’s best bakeries!

Nice modern cottage for 1- 5 people with garden, BBQ, fireplace, animals dom. for 10€ per day. We have bicycles for renting. Near the house is a beautiful forest and fresh air. You can go jogging or strolling with your dog.
Parking is free. In sommer time we have a swimming pool. By arrival please pay city tax , facilities for gas, water and electricity (about 3€ pro person ans day).
neighborhood_overview: I like this accomodation because it is near Center of Berlin and you have a beautiful garden. It is an arbor filled location. You are the Feeling like on the village, but you are in few minutes by car or train in the metropolitan life. You can see in Berlin-Karlshorst a lot of historical places, Horse races, museum of kapitulation...
host_about:
Ich freue mich auf Gäste aus der ganzen Welt und spreche außerDeutsch etwas Englisch, Russisch und ein wenig Polnisch. Demnächst wird auch noch Französisch dazukommen. Diese schöne Sprache lerne ich gerade. Ich spiele Klavier und habe auch 2 Geigen, die auf Spieler warten.
Als Lehrerin für Deutsch kann ich euch helfen, diese Sprache zu verbessern.  
Meine Fächer sind außerdem Chemie und Biologie. 
Ich helfe Abiturienten oder auch jüngeren Schülern mit Nachhilfeunterricht in Deutsch und Nawi.
In unserem großen Garten können wir Tisch-Tennis spielen oder am offenen Kamin träumen.
Das hängt natürlich ab von der Jahreszeit ab. Allerdings haben wir es schon geschafft, eine Sibirische Gartenparty zu feiern.
Mein Leitspruch ist: Tun und Ruh'n in rechtem Grade.
Und denkt daran: Der frühe Vogel fängt den Wurm!

neighborhood_overview: Known for its wide boulevards and multifaceted architecture, Schöneberg is a colorful district that’s home to an unlikely mix of affluent, middle class, immigrant and expat, and LGBTQ residents of all ages. Vibrant outdoor markets, a wide range of vintage and luxury shopping, an eclectic food scene, charming cafes and bars, and lively street festivals all contribute to its rich neighborhood culture. As dynamic as this district can be, peace and quiet are easy to find, thanks to plenty of parks and sedate streets awash in old-world glamor.

description: Show up and start living from day one in Berlin with this pretty two bedroom Blueground apartment. You’ll love coming home to this thoughtfully furnished, beautifully designed, and fully-equipped Kreuzberg home . (ID #BER288)
neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Kreuzberg, a sprawling neighborhood in central Berlin. The dynamic and fast-growing area is popular with students, young professionals, and new arrivals to the city. You will find cafes and eateries at every price point, as well as an abundance of Turkish restaurants around Kottbusser Tor. Bergmannkiez is known for both its 19th century architecture and the cool independent stores and abundant restaurants and bars along Bergmanstrasse. Kreuzberg is home to several notable museums, including the Berlinische Gallery, the German Museum of Technology and the Jewish Museum Berlin. Other highlights include the famous Checkpoint Charlie, located along the neighborhood's northern border, and Potsdamer Platz. Kreuzberg is served by several S-Bahn, U-Bahn and bus lines.

This elegant neighborhood is known for its historic architecture and high-end shopping scene, and there's no shortage of things to see and do nearby. description: Discover the best of Berlin, with this two bedroom apartment in Kreuzberg. . It’ll be easy to simply show up and start living in this airy Blueground furnished apartment with its fully-equipped kitchen, stylish living room, and our dedicated, on-the-ground support. (ID #BER295)
neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Kreuzberg, a sprawling neighborhood in central Berlin. The dynamic and fast-growing area is popular with students, young professionals, and new arrivals to the city. You will find cafes and eateries at every price point, as well as an abundance of Turkish restaurants around Kottbusser Tor. Bergmannkiez is known for both its 19th century architecture and the cool independent stores and abundant restaurants and bars along Bergmanstrasse. Kreuzberg is home to several notable museums, including the Berlinische Gallery, the German Museum of Technology and the Jewish Museum Berlin. Other highlights include the famous Checkpoint Charlie, located along the neighborhood's northern border, and Potsdamer Platz. Kreuzberg is served by several S-Bahn, U-Bahn and bus lines.
host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

neighborhood_overview:
Spandau is the fourth largest district of Berlin with 91,91 km², but has the smallest population of the 12 districts. Spandau is older than Berlin.
Spandau Altstadt invites to go shopping or to stop off for some food or drink, you can visit the Spandau Citadel close by or enjoy beautiful walks along the Havel, or to the island Eiswerder, in the Spandau forest or the nature reserve around the "Kiesteich" (has a little beach). Lots of nature in Spandau... for guests with a car (or bike) it's also nice to go towards Kladow or Gatow, also very much suitable for long walks along the water or taking the ferry crossing over the Havel to Wannsee.
From Hakenfelde/Aalemannufer there is another ferry to Tegel.
The former border strip on the edge of Spandau is good for biking tours, maybe taking a break in the adjoing villages Falkensee or Schönwalde (which also have a lake in which swimming is possible).

During re-construction a lot of attention has been spend on details and only ecological materials have been used.

The metro (U8) and the Ring-S-Bahn are right in front of the house so you can reach Alenxanderplatz in 15min. All windows are sound proof (triple glazing).
neighborhood_overview:
You will be located in the heart of Berlin´s most trendy district of Neukölln. In walking distance you can find shops for your daily convenience, trendy bars, amazing restaurants, newly opened galleries (Kindl Brauerei) and also one of the largest parks in Berlin. This park is the former city airport of Berlin called Tempelhof. It was the first civic airport in the world and now a 330ha large oasis. The former runways are perfect for joking in the morning, skating or cycling.

Two large supermarkets are just on the opposite site of the road. Within 5min walking distance you find everything from bakery, 4th generation coffee shop, Asia market and burger shops.

Many off license shops in the area are open 24h.

neighborhood_overview: Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf is the main part of West-Berlin. It borders on Mitte in the east, Tempelhof-Schöneberg in the southeast, Steglitz-Zehlendorf in the south, Spandau in the west and Reinickendorf in the north. The district includes the inner city localities of Charlottenburg, Wilmersdorf and Halensee. After World War II and the city's division by the Berlin Wall, the area around Kurfürstendamm and Bahnhof Zoo was the centre of former West Berlin, with the Kaiser Wilhelm Memorial Church as its landmark. The Berlin Institute of Technology (Technische Universität Berlin), the Berlin University of the Arts (Universität der Künste), the Federal Institute for Risk Assessment (Bundesinstitut für Risikobewertung), the Deutsche Oper Berlin as well as Charlottenburg Palace and the Olympic Stadium are also located in Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf.

description:

neighborhood_overview:
Located between Prenzlauerberg + Friedrichshain on the M10 Tram Line.

Nestled in the heart of Berlin, Friedrichshain pulsates with creativity and energy. This eclectic borough seamlessly fuses historic charm with a modern, artsy vibe. Cobblestone streets lead to an array of trendy cafes, quirky boutiques, and vibrant street art, creating an atmosphere that captivates locals and visitors alike.

Home to iconic locations like the East Side Gallery—an open-air gallery adorned with remnants of the Berlin Wall—Friedrichshain tells a story of the city's rich history and its dynamic present. As day turns to night, the neighborhood transforms into a nightlife hub, boasting an array of bars and clubs that keep the revelry alive until the early morning hours.

name: Spend the night in the oldest house in Berlin!
description: Here you will be sleeping in the oldest residential building in Berlin from the 17th century, the only worldly building left from the extension of the medieval town, with original architecture on its original site - even if you would hardly notice it ...
neighborhood_overview:
You really will live in the oldest residential building in Berlin - even if you would hardly know it, since a Mediterranean style outer facade was added during renovation in the nineties and even native Berliners and walkers pass it unwittingly every day. So do not be surprised if a group with an experienced city guide stops to look up at the house. You are in a 100% listed building.

A 17th century building "lives" and "moves" - so you are not afraid of creaking floorboards or the like - it is certainly not the ghosts of old times ... adjoining street noise, tourists or sometimes a night-time act on the street or a passing police car, let you know despite well-insulated windows, that you are still in the Berlin of the 21st century.

As well as part of the city wall, the oldest dwelling-house and the oldest restaurant, Waisenstrasse also has Berlin's oldest cemetery - the Parochialfriedhof - as well as the ruins of the old Franciscan monastery. All other sights

The area is incredibly rich in history, with many of the city's museums and tourist sites located within the neighborhood. Bustling Mitte is one of the city's key business districts, and there is a diverse restaurant scene with options at all price points. You can splash out at a high-end restaurant like Schnitzelei Mitte, or head to trendy Hackescher Markt, where cafes and shops are located within a series of labyrinthine courtyards. Mitte is also wonderful for sightseeing: the Brandenburg Gate and Museum Island are both located within the neighborhood. Other highlights include the famous Checkpoint Charlie, Tiergarten Park and the Berlin Zoo. There’s also the historic Tiergarten, one of Germany’s largest public parks and the perfect place for a dose of nature. neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Mitte, Berlin's first and most central district. The area is incredibly rich in history, with many of the city's museums and tourist sites located within the neighborhood. Bustling Mitte is one of the city's key business districts, and there is a diverse restaurant scene with options at all price points. You can splash out at a high-end restaurant like Schnitzelei Mitte, or head to trendy Hackescher Markt, where cafes and shops are located within a series of labyrinthine courtyards. Mitte is also wonderful for sightseeing: the Brandenburg Gate and Museum Island are both located within the neighborhood. Other highlights include the famous Checkpoint Charlie, Tiergarten Park and the Berlin Zoo. There’s also the historic Tiergarten, one of Germany’s largest public parks and the perfect place for a dose of nature. Another point of interest is Moabit, formerly an important border between East and West Berlin. Berlin's central train station is located i

The neighborhood is known for its cool boutiques and after hours club scene. There's also a diverse culinary scene, with everything from authentic Vietnamese food to traditional German pub fare on offer. Friedrichshain is home to the East Side Gallery, part of the Berlin Wall that has been preserved for posterity. Other area highlights include the Computerspiele Museum, where you can explore the evolution of the modern computer, and Mercedes-Benz Arena Berlin, where you can catch all the biggest acts in the music world when they come to town. Friedrichshain is served by several S-Bahn, U-Bahn and bus lines.

Berlin ist immer eine Reise wert. Umso schöner ist es und das ist zumindest unsere Meinung, andere Länder und Städte so zu bereisen, dass man das Lebensgefühl der Einheimischen etwas einfängt. Und was eignet sich denn dafür besser, als eine Unterkunft in einer ruhigen und wunderschönen Wohngegend?

Es handelt sich um keine Partywohnung, es ist ein ruhiges Haus und wir nehmen aufeinander Rücksicht. Es ist kein Fernseher vorhanden, da ich keinen benutzen. Es ist ein Bett 90 x 200 sowie eine ausklappbare Schlafcouch 160 x 200 vorhanden. Nähere Informationen gerne auf Anfrage.

neighborhood_overview: Cosmopolitan Neukölln is popular with artists, students and new arrivals in Berlin. The neighborhood is known for its large international population and busy streets lined with Middle Eastern pastry shops and restaurants. Nature lovers won't want to miss Britzer Garten, a lovely park with frequent flower shows. Neukölln's Turkish Market is the perfect place to shop for pottery, rugs, spices and fresh produce. Other neighborhood highlights include the Schloss Britz, an 18th century mansion that is now a museum, and quaint Rixdorf, a charming village-like area that's perfect for an afternoon walk. Neukölln has excellent U-Bahn access and several bus lines.

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

name: Modern rooftop flat with terrace 70sqm
description: Bright, spacious and modern top floor flat in the middle of Berlin, only a 3-minute walk from the U6 line. One bedroom with king-sized bed, leading out to a large combined living and kitchen area with huge ceilings and wooden flooring throughout. Enjoy the Berlin sunset from the balcony, or explore the area which was once right on the wall dividing the former East and West. A third guest can be accommodated on the couch. There is NO lift, so not suited to those with limited mobility.
neighborhood_overview: Although situated in a quiet little corner of Mitte, this area has really developed in the last 5 years. Lots of new apartments springing up on the main street as well as Cafes and supermarkets. 19 Grams Cafe has the best coffee and brunch options by far. Head further north to Naturkundemuseum for more food options as well as (naturally) the Natural Science Museum which has the world's best preserved Tyrannosaurus Rex (called Tristan!). The Brandenburg gate is a bike ride away, fragments of the Berlin wall just 10 minutes walk, and the museum there is worth a look. There's a long park nearby which is full of beautiful birch trees, and further north is another upcoming area called 'Wedding' where you can walk along the canal and find quirky bars and even an old piano tuning warehouse which puts on nightly classical music concerts.
host_about: You love to travel, or else you wouldn't be on nocarz! Same goes for me. I work in media and have seen quite a lot of the world, but happy to see way more. I don't mind the odd glass of wine, or mezcal cocktail. I'm sociable and enjoy the company of mostly everyone. I like hearing your stories, and have a few of my own. Looking forward to meeting all of those intrepid adventurous people out there!

neighborhood_overview:
Kreuzberg is arguably the most exciting neighbourhood in Berlin. All the hipster trends tend to start here. It's the home to a bustling multicultural and young community that work at start-ups and have cocktails at understated bars in the evening. It's a dynamic and lively place, where well-educated people that value concepts like fair trading and organic shopping like to hang out or live in.

Kreuzberg is quintessentially Berliner in joining the bourgeois charm of the West with the socially-conscious ideologies of the East. It's a wonderful place to live in. We'll be happy to share it with you.

name: Dein Zuhause in Friedrichshain
description:
Beautiful quiet old building apartment in Friedrichshain, near the Spree. Wonderfully suitable as a starting point for all city travellers. After an exciting day in Berlin, there is enough space in the apartment ( 3-room, 89 sqm) to relax and raise your legs or explore the culinary diversity of the Simon Dach Kiezes in a few minutes' walk, which is known for its vibrant atmosphere. Historical significance, creativity and alternative flair are combined here.
Enjoy and relax like at home!
neighborhood_overview:
The location of the apartment in Friedrichshain is ideal. To the right out the front door you are in 2 minutes on the Spree, overlooking the Molecule Man.
If you take the left down Danneckerstraße, you are directly at Rudolfplatz, where there is a Späti (kiosk), which fulfills some first wishes directly.
Also two restaurants when the hunger is high and the path to the Simon Dach Kiez too far. (10 min walk). There are a variety of restaurants and bars, small boutiques, hip ice cream parlors, cool Berlin pubs that are open into the morning hours or alternative clubs on the well-known raw grounds.
Also within walking distance you can reach the East Side Gallery and the Oberbaum Bridge, which takes you directly to lively Kreuzberg.
If you prefer peace and nature, you can take a nice walk along the Spree into Treptower Park, past the picturesque island of youth or the sailing ship restaurant "Klipper", where you can enjoy delicious food, even during the day.

neighborhood_overview: See Travelers ressources
host_about:
I made my first trip to Berlin in dec. 1988, just one year before the wall fell down. I started to go there more regularly 10 years ago and it soon became obvious that I needed my own place there : stressless life, friendly and open minded people, solidarity and kindness in every behavior, I found there exactly what I needed after 17 years living in Paris (…).

I bought my apartment end of february 2012 and started to renew it all by myself. It is not yet fully decorated, but already comfortable and I’m proud of what I made of it. If you come several times, you will notice the changes all through the years.

I any case, I’ve tried to make it the more comfortable I can, for myself, but also for my guest, so that we can all have a real home while discovering Berlin.

Hope to hear from you soon.
Yves

host_about:
I am mainly a solo female traveller around the world (I especially like to travel alone by bus to south America) where I use to visit my friends or just make new ones who unfit normal schemes, like artists and musicians.
That's how I learned languages "on the road" but I am also graduated at the Alma Mater University of Bologna (Italy)  in German Linguistic and Literature
I have been living in Berlin for the last over 30 years, as I arrived here exactly one year before the fall of the Wall, which is few steps from my door. 
According to my degree I would be a translator and language teacher but in my 30 years in Berlin I have renovated houses, organized punk concerts and lived by squatters and vagrants.  
I am vegan but you can eat what you want in my place and store in my fridge. 
I could take you on special tours,  showing you how real Berlin was before gentrification or just to have a beer in a ungentrifyable kreuzberger kneipe or take you to a club, experiencing the real city as you will never  find on trip advisor.  
I like to be a tourist in my hometown.

neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Friedrichshain, a hip neighborhood in the eastern part of Berlin. The neighborhood is known for its cool boutiques and after hours club scene. There's also a diverse culinary scene, with everything from authentic Vietnamese food to traditional German pub fare on offer. Friedrichshain is home to the East Side Gallery, part of the Berlin Wall that has been preserved for posterity. Other area highlights include the Computerspiele Museum, where you can explore the evolution of the modern computer, and Mercedes-Benz Arena Berlin, where you can catch all the biggest acts in the music world when they come to town. Friedrichshain is served by several S-Bahn, U-Bahn and bus lines.

description: Show up and start living from day one in Berlin with this lovely one bedroom Blueground apartment. You’ll love coming home to this thoughtfully furnished, beautifully designed, and fully-equipped Neukölln home . (ID #BER176)
neighborhood_overview: Cosmopolitan Neukölln is popular with artists, students and new arrivals in Berlin. The neighborhood is known for its large international population and busy streets lined with Middle Eastern pastry shops and restaurants. Nature lovers won't want to miss Britzer Garten, a lovely park with frequent flower shows. Neukölln's Turkish Market is the perfect place to shop for pottery, rugs, spices and fresh produce. Other neighborhood highlights include the Schloss Britz, an 18th century mansion that is now a museum, and quaint Rixdorf, a charming village-like area that's perfect for an afternoon walk. Neukölln has excellent U-Bahn access and several bus lines.
host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

The area is incredibly rich in history, with many of the city's museums and tourist sites located within the neighborhood. Bustling Mitte is one of the city's key business districts, and there is a diverse restaurant scene with options at all price points. You can splash out at a high-end restaurant like Schnitzelei Mitte, or head to trendy Hackescher Markt, where cafes and shops are located within a series of labyrinthine courtyards. Mitte is also wonderful for sightseeing: the Brandenburg Gate and Museum Island are both located within the neighborhood. Other highlights include the famous Checkpoint Charlie, Tiergarten Park and the Berlin Zoo. There’s also the historic Tiergarten, one of Germany’s largest public parks and the perfect place for a dose of nature. name: Stylish well furnished large room in Mitte
description: Freshly renovated room in a light flooded stylish three room apartment in the absolute centre of Berlin Mitte. As I myself just moved to Berlin the furniture is completely new and awaits your visit. The Apartment is situated in a quiet side street of the Torstraße, halfway between Rosenthaler Platz and Rosa-Luxenburg-Platz. If you stay here, you are sure to make the most out of your Berlin experience no matter if you are a foody, party animal or artsy - you are welcomed.
neighborhood_overview:
The neighborhood is a true gem. With cafés and restaurants reaching all over the place starting with the bigger more tourist ones on Torstraße and finishing of with the small cozy ones where an old italian is preaching the menu of his mind. There's something for everyones tastebuds.
If you are looking for some night life experience Mitte has so much to offer. I personally am more of a 'chilled bar drink and talk' kind of guy especially in bars that you wouldn't even find if you were looking for them. But there're definitely some clubs in the area which are worth visiting.
host_about:
Originally born in Berlin my mom and I moved to Hamburg when I was four years old, where I practically grew up. Being raised by the motto "no human can only live in extremes - neither sport nor philosophy, work nor party, social nor reclusive" lead me to a diversity of interests which I am still living today ranging from a variety of sports through a rather mixed music taste up to a culinary 'foody'. 

I love travelling and actually started to step out of my own country way earlier than I can remember. For me rather than visiting all the sights (, which obviously is a MUST), actually experiencing the culture and the way of people living in it is way more interesting. That's the reason why I hate it when I am in a country I don't speak the language of, which probably led to my experiences abroad in Canada and Spain, where I lived in a year each.

name: Centre Quiet 2 Room Flat WiFi, Anmeldung
description: A very quiet apartment in the historical centre of Berlin, ca. 8 min. walk to the Main Railway Station (Hauptbahnhof). Several main museums in a walking distance. One bedroom, 50m²,old building.Huge garden. Excellent transportation! No stairsclimbing
neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is located in historical part of the very centre of the old city. The 3 stories buildings around the area were originally designed for the army veterans of the French-Prussian war, mostly for those, who couldn't afford to pay the rent to stay in the city. Later the area was populated with the doctors, nurses and students of the Charité, the medical faculty of the Humboldt University of Berlin.

Most of the most important museums (Museum Island, Brandenburger Gate, Hamburger Bahnhof, Reichstag) are located within 5 to 30 min. walking.

In 70 meters distance from the house it used to be the boarder between East and West Berlin.

In case of medical emergency - 24h/7d ER of the University Clinic Charité is in 5 minute walk.
host_about:
I work as a medical doctor and this is the summer apartment, that we rent sometimes. According to the new rules of the government of Berlin, being inforced from the 1th May 2016, we cannot rent the flat for the period of time less than 220 days. In the past we were accepting stays of at least 7 days, so we collected a lot of references, that you are more than welcome to read.
Our intention in the moment are the longer term contracts.

name: Alcove by the Spree
description: My street is a quiet alcove but at its end are the immaculate parks and gardens of the Schloss Charlottenburg with lush jogging and biking paths stretched around the Spree.  There are oodles of supermarkets within walking distance and an outdoor weekly market around the corner.  The bus, U-bahn, and ring are all 5-10 minutes by foot.  The deep greens of Tiergarten, Volkspark Jungfernheide, and Forst Grunewald are a long walk or a short bus ride away.
neighborhood_overview:
My block is mostly sleepy and residential but sprinkled with amazing eateries; within a short walk, exist a variety of neighborhood cafes, spätis, ice cream shops, and restaurants.

At the end of the street, a lush green umbrage saddles the Spree with parks on either side: the extensive gardens beside the Schloss, or in the other direction, playgrounds, willow trees, and humans lazing on the riverbank. The walking, biking, and jogging paths are prima and seemingly endless.

Just around the corner from the flat, on Wednesday and Saturday mornings, there is the farmer's market, Wochenmarkt am Mierendorffplatz. Volkspark Jungfernheide is within walking distance and Forst Grunewald is a short jaunt by bike or public transportation. The Buddhist center, Dharma Mati is one of the areas gems. The rich foliage of the west along with some of Berlin's best lakes make for an ideal day-trip.

You can splash out at a high-end restaurant like Schnitzelei Mitte, or head to trendy Hackescher Markt, where cafes and shops are located within a series of labyrinthine courtyards. Mitte is also wonderful for sightseeing: the Brandenburg Gate and Museum Island are both located within the neighborhood. Other highlights include the famous Checkpoint Charlie, Tiergarten Park and the Berlin Zoo. In the past years the gravy train that is Kreuzkölln has extended its reach to this nice little neighborhood. It is characterized by peaceful roads and pre-war tenement houses, which set the stage for a harmonious mix of young families, international alternative lifestyle enterprises, and the organically grown Neukölln immigrant communities. Many lovely little cafés, restaurants and the massive free space of the Airfield Park attract people from all over town, creating a colorful yet relaxed urban vibe.

neighborhood_overview: Cosmopolitan Neukölln is popular with artists, students and new arrivals in Berlin. The neighborhood is known for its large international population and busy streets lined with Middle Eastern pastry shops and restaurants. Nature lovers won't want to miss Britzer Garten, a lovely park with frequent flower shows. Neukölln's Turkish Market is the perfect place to shop for pottery, rugs, spices and fresh produce. Other neighborhood highlights include the Schloss Britz, an 18th century mansion that is now a museum, and quaint Rixdorf, a charming village-like area that's perfect for an afternoon walk. Neukölln has excellent U-Bahn access and several bus lines.

With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily.

Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Kreuzberg, a sprawling neighborhood in central Berlin. The dynamic and fast-growing area is popular with students, young professionals, and new arrivals to the city. You will find cafes and eateries at every price point, as well as an abundance of Turkish restaurants around Kottbusser Tor. Bergmannkiez is known for both its 19th century architecture and the cool independent stores and abundant restaurants and bars along Bergmanstrasse. Kreuzberg is home to several notable museums, including the Berlinische Gallery, the German Museum of Technology and the Jewish Museum Berlin. Other highlights include the famous Checkpoint Charlie, located along the neighborhood's northern border, and Potsdamer Platz. Kreuzberg is served by several S-Bahn, U-Bahn and bus lines.
host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Charlottenburg, which is set within the district of Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf in Berlin's inner city. This elegant neighborhood is known for its historic architecture and high-end shopping scene, and there's no shortage of things to see and do nearby. Charlottenburg Palace, a Baroque castle that is now a museum, is worth a visit. Other neighborhood highlights include the Deutsche Oper Berlin and Schaubühne, a three-stage Modernist theater designed by Erich Mendelsohn. There's also the lovely Forst Grunewald, a vast greenspace located along the western border of the neighborhood. Charlottenburg is served by several U-Bahn and bus lines.
host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

name: Cozy green private room in central location
description:
I‘m renting out my spacious room in Wedding during the summer vacations. It is cozy, quiet and centrally located with good access to a range of public transport methods. It has a heating system with integrated fan (blows hot air into the room!), lots of cute plants, fast WiFi and a good set of speakers.
Since it‘s part of a shared flat, kitchen and bathroom will be shared with my flatmate, who is 26 and at work most of the time.
neighborhood_overview:
Close to my place are a tiny river, alongside which it is very green and you can have nice walks, two artist spaces (Panke Club & Gallery and Savvy Contemporary) that host exhibitions, bar evenings and club events, the beautiful and big Humboldthain park with a platform for a view over Berlin, and of course an abundance of restaurants, Spätis (late night shops) and supermarkets. All of the above are reachable by foot in less than 10, some in less than 5 minutes.
The district Wedding is culturally very diverse and offers a variety of entertainment, but the connection to other districts via public transport is still very good (30-45min to Kreuzberg/Neukölln, e.g. for clubbing or long bar nights), as there are many important tube and train lines close by.
host_about: I‘m a travel enthusiast and music lover, living in Berlin since 8 years. I am currently working on my PhD in medicine .

Although there is a disco (Havana) nearby, most prefer to go to the Späti or around the corner to the Altberliner Otto Schruppke. Also highly recommended: the hose pub Felsenkeller. This area in former East Berlin is exceptionally beautiful with carefully renovated buildings, tree lined streets, small parks and hidden courtyards. It is quiet if you want your peace and a good night sleep. At the same time there are plenty of excellent bakeries, restaurants and cozy bars nearby.
For history buffs the Berlin wall memorial is close by. So are the lively Kastanienallee and Oderbergerstrasse where you find small shops with vintage clothing, books and other gems. The weekly flea markets at Mauerpark and Arkonaplatz are right around the corner as are two arthouse cinemas.

You can splash out at a high-end restaurant like Schnitzelei Mitte, or head to trendy Hackescher Markt, where cafes and shops are located within a series of labyrinthine courtyards. Mitte is also wonderful for sightseeing: the Brandenburg Gate and Museum Island are both located within the neighborhood. Other highlights include the famous Checkpoint Charlie, Tiergarten Park and the Berlin Zoo. name: Lovely Apartment Treptower Park
description:
Charming old building apartment with Berlin flair in the countryside.
It has 65 sqm with 1 room
and an equipped kitchen.
Apartment is a 15-minute walk from Treptower Park S-Bahn Station, where you can quickly reach every destination by public transport. Not only a green view offers the park in front of the door, but also numerous possibilities. From pedal boat riding on the Spree, cafes in the park, Lostplaces such as the Spreepark to the Soviet Honorary Monument.
neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is close to the Innstadt and in the middle of the countryside.
S- Train station Treptower Park is only 1 km away and is easily accessible on foot with a walk in the countryside and on the water. If you are in a hurry, there is a 1 minute walk away, including the bus station. The connection with public transport is very good, as you can easily reach any destination by ring train. Just around the corner you will find a supermarket, as well as good restaurants and cafes at the harbor in the park opposite. There are some attractions in the immediate vicinity. This is a perfect place for all visitors who want to experience Berlin on all trains, whether the green Berlin, the Berlin afterlife or the historic Berlin, everyone gets their money's worth here. There is free public parking in the area.
More information and accurate addresses are included in our digital guidebooks. You can find these in our listing.

name: Blueground | Friedrichshain, furnished
description: Discover the best of Berlin, with this two bedroom apartment in Friedrichshain. . It’ll be easy to simply show up and start living in this beautifully Blueground furnished apartment with its fully-equipped kitchen, stylish living room, and our dedicated, on-the-ground support. (ID #BER247)
neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Friedrichshain, a hip neighborhood in the eastern part of Berlin. The neighborhood is known for its cool boutiques and after hours club scene. There's also a diverse culinary scene, with everything from authentic Vietnamese food to traditional German pub fare on offer. Friedrichshain is home to the East Side Gallery, part of the Berlin Wall that has been preserved for posterity. Other area highlights include the Computerspiele Museum, where you can explore the evolution of the modern computer, and Mercedes-Benz Arena Berlin, where you can catch all the biggest acts in the music world when they come to town. Friedrichshain is served by several S-Bahn, U-Bahn and bus lines.
host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

description:
Experience Berlin Neukölln and a high level of comfort in this quietly located studio apartment:

The floor heating ensures warm feet throughout the apartment. Not to mention the stylish bathroom with a luxurious rain shower, that can keep up with any boutique hotel! The king-size bed will give you a good night's sleep. There's an elevator in the building, and shopping facilities as well as the underground and S-Bahn are right outside the door!
neighborhood_overview:
Neukölln is one of Berlin's most vibrant and culturally rich neighborhoods! Nestled in the southeastern part of the city, Neukölln offers an eclectic blend of old-world charm and modern urban energy.

The lively mix of cafes, boutiques, and art galleries, is all set against the backdrop of beautifully preserved Altbau architecture. Highlights like Körnerpark and the vast Tempelhofer Feld are perfect for picnics, leisurely walks, and outdoor activities.

If you're a foodie, you'll feel right at home in the diverse culinary scene. From cozy corner bistros to international eateries, Neukölln serves up a tantalizing array of flavors. And when the sun sets, the neighborhood comes alive with a thriving nightlife, with an abundance of bars, clubs, and live music venues.

The area is known for its relatively young population, and is especially popular for those with young children. There are cafes and restaurants throughout the area, including notable international options like Wen Cheng Handpulled Noodles. Anyone who likes to cook will be pleased by the selection of local supermarkets and Prenzlauer Berg hosts a popular Sunday farmer's market at Mauerpark. The MACHmit! host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.

name: Cosy Sleep inn ~ Great area ✨
description:
My place is perfect for short stays and allows full privacy:

- Large, calm and comfortable room in a typical Berliner "Altbau" (ancient construction)
- Pretty neighborhood of Schöneberg, peaceful but very animated: restaurants, cafés, bars, supermarkets all in walking distance
- Many bus and metro stations: 4 subway stops away from Potsdamerplatz and Brandenburg Gate
- Clean and clear bathroom, fresh sheets, towels and soaps
- Lots of informations in the room about Berlin and the neighborhood
neighborhood_overview:
The neighborhood is amazing! I fell in love with it first time I saw it.
First, it is not too far from the Center: in my good days it takes me 15 minutes by bike to go to Potsdamer Platz. It is only 4 S-Bahn Stations away!
The street is very quiet and crowned by the extraordinary Gasometer (I love this Gasometer more than the Eiffel Tower!;) There's almost no cars passing by and still the neighborhood is unbelievably alive and animated a few minutes away;
There are many green spaces around and trees everywhere, it smells so good in the summer!
Everything is 2 steps away: subway and bus stops, shops, restaurants, bars and cafes, many Art galleries, outdoors markets...
For me (I experience it every day) Schöneberg is one of the best neighborhood of Berlin. Beautiful, charming, full of life and tolerant people... Here lived Marlene Dietrich and David Bowie and was born Albert Einstein ;) Here, John Fitzgerald Kennedy once said: "I am a Berliner!".

Lots of windows & sunshine, a spacious living room, a cute patio, a washer, and high-speed wifi + TV. The apt overlooks an occasionally lively, late-night street.

neighborhood_overview:
Formerly a neighborhood in East Berlin, Friedrichshain has an alternative and artistic vibe. Tourists are often attracted to the area’s famous GDR-era relics, including the longest surviving stretch of the Berlin Wall, the East Side Gallery, and the socialist boulevard, Karl-Marx-Allee. However, the artsy neighborhood is also known for its punk-locals, artistic collectives and for housing Berlin’s most rambunctious nightlife.

There are tons of great restaurants within a 5-minute walk of my apartment, a number of places to enjoy craft beer, great coffee, and green spaces! The neighborhood is very walkable and quiet at night.

name: Urban Life Studio, 40 qm
description:
Rest in the middle of it all overlooking the lively hustle and bustle at the S-Bahn station "Nordbahnhof" and the vibrant Invalidenstraße.
The large living area with workplace and the slightly separated sleeping area, the elegant bathroom and the fully equipped kitchen make this apartment the ideal temporary home in a compact space with a small loggia.

Available on floors 1, 3 & 5.
neighborhood_overview: Hardly any other place in Berlin combines authentic neighborhood life, the history of the once divided city and the vibrant, cosmopolitan spirit as attractive as in the Mitte district around the Invalidenstraße. World-class cultural highlights such as the Museum Island or the Natural History Museum are just as easy to reach as the numerous theatres and opera houses. The Berlin Wall Memorial is right on your doorstep. Guests can also discover a variety of bars, restaurants and breakfast options in the immediate vicinity. Popular attractions within walking distance are Torstraße, Oranienburger Straße, Rosenthaler Platz or Hackescher Markt. Numerous ministries, corporations, agencies and institutions are located in this dynamic environment, as is the Charité campus.
host_about: Die Downtown Apartments bieten 27 vollständig ausgestattete Serviced Apartments: vom 36 qm großen Studio über großzügige Wohnungen mit zwei Schlafzimmern und zwei Bädern bis zum 147 qm weiten Penthouse mit zwei traumhaften Terrassen. Jedes Apartment verfügt über eine Loggia oder eine Terrasse – mit Blick ins Grüne und Sichtachsen bis zum Fernsehturm am Alexanderplatz. Geeignet für Kurzaufenthalte ab 2 Nächten oder Langzeitaufenthalte für bis zu 6 Monate.

This family-run hotel is perfectly located in Berlin's Schöneberg district, just a 10-minute walk from the world-famous KaDeWe department store and Kurfürstendamm. Hotel Ambiente is a 5-minute walk from Nollendorfplatz U2 subway station. This offers direct connections to the Berlin ICC exhibition, the Olympic Stadium and Potsdamer Platz.

An underground parking garage is available at the Hotel Ambiente. The A100 motorway of the city of Berlin is only 3 km away.

We care about you-the customer service is our strength!

We organize your stay in the city of Berlin.

name: Bright & green Berlin original in Kreuzberg !
description: We are three students from Berlin and lost our heart in Kreuzberg. We live together in this large beautiful apartment directly at famous Mariannenplatz and have a room with parc view which we like to share with you!
neighborhood_overview:
You walk five minutes to the hip Oranienstrasse and Heinrichplatz. Nearby there are countless restaurants, bars and other places to go out. In the surrounding area you will find Berlin's most popular clubs, such as the famous SO36, Tresor, Berghain, etc.
There are many walks around Mariannenplatz. For example, you can go along the Kreuzberg Canal, the Engelbecken or along the old Berlin Wall area (East Side Gallery).
From a cultural point of view, our neighbourhood is definitely not missing out. Directly opposite our house is the Kulturhaus Bethanien, where there are regular exhibitions, park-restaurant, as well as a highly recommended open-air cinema.
The nearest shopping facilities are Markthalle 9 (5 minutes walk), an Aldi market directly connected to Markhalle, a Lidl market in Wrangelstraße and a Lidl market in Kottbuser Straße, REWE at Kottbusser Tor and REWE at Ostbahnhof (all 10 minutes walk).

name: Blueground | Charlottenburg, fully furnished
description: Feel at home wherever you choose to live with Blueground. You’ll love this sophisticated Charlottenburg furnished one bedroom apartment with its modern decor, fully equipped kitchen, and pretty living room with great views. Ideally located, you’re close to all the best that Berlin has to offer! (ID #BER261)
neighborhood_overview: This furnished apartment is located in Charlottenburg, which is set within the district of Charlottenburg-Wilmersdorf in Berlin's inner city. This elegant neighborhood is known for its historic architecture and high-end shopping scene, and there's no shortage of things to see and do nearby. Charlottenburg Palace, a Baroque castle that is now a museum, is worth a visit. Other neighborhood highlights include the Deutsche Oper Berlin and Schaubühne, a three-stage Modernist theater designed by Erich Mendelsohn. There's also the lovely Forst Grunewald, a vast greenspace located along the western border of the neighborhood. Charlottenburg is served by several U-Bahn and bus lines.
host_about:
We’re Blueground, a global proptech company with several thousand move-in-ready apartments in a growing number of major cities around the world. With flexible terms and homes in vibrant, centrally based neighborhoods, you’ll feel at home and free to roam for as long as you want — a month, a year, or longer. 

Each apartment is thoughtfully designed with exclusive furnishings, fully equipped kitchens, and incredible amenities – making every day a five-star experience. From day one, you’ll enjoy high-speed Wi-Fi, premium linens, and smart home entertainment. Plus, access to pools, gyms, and outdoor spaces in select buildings.

Why stress over your apartment? We provide a hassle-free alternative — a consistent, quality guest experience that starts even before you arrive. Because we let you book our most up-to-date apartment listings online, confirm with a click, pay securely, and check in easily. 

During your stay
Upon arrival, you’ll either be greeted personally by a Blueground team member or given self-check-in instructions. The entire apartment is yours! You’ll enjoy reliable support via email, phone, and our Guest App, where you can request everything from a home cleaning to extra towels. 

We’ll share all details upon confirmation of your stay.
